run_type: manual kickoff — operator-requested, outside normal daily cadence
headline: Domain migration: canonical URL moved from funwithscience-org.github.io/dome-model-review to funwithscience.net/dome-model-review. All deployed machine-readable surfaces (canonical, og:url, sitemap, robots, ClaimReview url/author.url, apex umbrella) verified live on the new canonical. Old URL issues HTTP 301. Four operator-hand follow-ups listed in the checklist at the top of this report.

human_notes_consumed.action_summary: Items 3 (discoverability-baseline.json URL + url_history + github_repo_metadata stale-note) and 5 (search-rankings.json funwithscience.net queries + migration_note) self-executed. Items 1 (GSC Domain property verification), 2 (Bing Webmaster Tools — retry after DNS TXT retry tomorrow), 4 (GitHub repo homepage field update), and 6 (ClaimReview schema validator re-check) listed as operator-hand actions in the checklist at the top of this report. Items 7 (outreach readiness) and 8 (do-not-touch list) noted for future reference — no action taken.

operator_action_checklist.description: These items require an interactive browser/account session with operator credentials. Listed at the top per SOCIAL-NOTE-004 item 'REPORTING'. Social cannot execute these from an agent run.

items.action: Google Search Console — verify funwithscience.net as a Domain property
items.why: New canonical URL needs its own GSC property so we can submit the sitemap and push URL Inspection. The existing funwithscience-org.github.io property should be kept verified (do NOT remove it) so it can collect redirect signals while Google consolidates authority.
steps: 1. Log into https://search.google.com/search-console with the operator Google account.
2. Add a new property → Domain property → enter 'funwithscience.net'.
3. Follow the DNS TXT verification flow via GoDaddy. Add the TXT record GSC provides at the DNS apex.
4. After verification, Sitemaps → submit 'https://funwithscience.net/sitemap.xml' AND 'https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/sitemap.xml'.
5. URL Inspection → enter 'https://funwithscience.net/' → Request Indexing.
6. URL Inspection → enter 'https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/' → Request Indexing.
7. Keep the existing funwithscience-org.github.io property verified indefinitely to receive redirect signals.
items.rough_time: ~15–20 min; DNS TXT propagation may add 0–60 min wait

items.action: Bing Webmaster Tools — verify funwithscience.net (retry after DNS TXT re-propagation)
items.why: Per operator note, GoDaddy DNS TXT did not verify on first attempt; retry planned for tomorrow (2026-04-22). Bing is ~5–8% ceiling on US share but gives us IndexNow push capability and Change-of-Address tooling.
steps: 1. Log into https://www.bing.com/webmasters with the operator account.
2. Add a site → Domain → enter 'funwithscience.net'.

3. DNS TXT verification via GoDaddy. If still failing, try the HTML meta tag method (we can emit it via a decider patch to build-scripts/generate-html.js if needed — file a request and social will draft).
4. After verification, submit sitemap 'https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/sitemap.xml'.
5. If a prior Bing property existed for funwithscience-org.github.io, use the Change of Address tool to point at the new domain.
items.note: If verification still fails after DNS TXT retry, flag for next report and we will draft an HTML-meta verification patch.

items.action: Update GitHub repo homepage field on funwithscience-org/dome-model-review
items.why: Currently still 'https://funwithscience-org.github.io/dome-model-review/' per public API this run. GitHub uses the homepage field as a crawl hint and displays it in the repo About panel. Stale URL here means external link traffic may follow the old path and rely on the 301 unnecessarily.
steps: Via gh CLI (recommended):
gh api -X PATCH repos/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review -f homepage='https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/'
Or via web UI:
https://github.com/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review → Settings (About panel gear icon) → Website field → paste the new URL → Save.

items.action: Validate ClaimReview JSON-LD against the new canonical URL
items.why: Low-risk check. Only 'url' and 'author.url' fields changed in the migration; 'itemReviewed.url' still correctly points at the dome site (the subject of review). But ClaimReview is the structured-data route Google uses for fact-check surfacing, so a quick validator run confirms no parser errors.
steps: 1. Open https://validator.schema.org/
2. 'Fetch URL' → paste 'https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/'
3. Confirm ClaimReview shows no errors and all expected fields resolve.
4. If any errors, flag in a new human-note and social will draft a corresponding patch to build-scripts/generate-html.js.

human_notes_check: SOCIAL-NOTE-001 (rebrand-resistance), -002 (indexing strategy), -003 (auto-detection signals) remain consumed. SOCIAL-NOTE-004 consumed this run (see action_summary above). No other pending notes.

author_activity.summary: Re-verified this run (a few hours after run1). Most-recent 10 commits remain exclusively scheduled bots (GitHub Action 'Auto-update ECM status' x6, ECM Auto-Pipeline 'data pull' x2, plus older entries). No new Nhughes09 commits — latest Nhughes09 commit still 2026-04-17T23:31:21Z (~4 days silence). Repo metadata still null (description, homepage, topics all empty).

Targeted site:funwithscience.net probes run. Google has exactly ONE funwithscience.net page indexed: https://funwithscience.net/product/wimshurst-machine/ — a legacy education-products listing from a prior occupant of the domain. The dome review path (funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/) is not yet indexed (Day 0 of new canonical). This is informational, not alarming: the legacy path is unrelated to dome content and won't confuse dome-model queries. Listed in search-rankings.json run2 for transparency.

No incremental TikTok/Twitter/Reddit/YouTube probe for run2 (operator-requested kickoff is discoverability-focused; author-activity coverage from run1 remains current — no posts, no vocabulary hits, 15 days running).

claim_overlap.source: Inherited from run1 — Journal of Geocentric Cosmology (Firmament GRIN), ANIMA Framework, thirteen.space
claim_overlap.summary: No change to claim-overlap landscape in the ~4 hours since run1. Migration does not affect third-party content.

github_activity.open_issue_note: Cloudflare Workers bot PR from 2026-03-06 (46 days stale). Only open issue.
github_activity.notable: Unchanged vs run1 apart from newer scheduled-bot timestamps. No new human commits; repo metadata still null. 2026-04-17 burst continues to look like a one-off AI-steering investment.

discoverability.competitive_assessment: OUR migration completed; DOME continues to stand still on author-side infrastructure. All deployed machine-readable surfaces on our side verified live on funwithscience.net: canonical link, og:url, ClaimReview url/author.url, sitemap, robots, llms.txt (URL-invariant content), and an apex umbrella at funwithscience.net/ with its own sitemap.xml + robots.txt + llms.txt. Old URL 301s correctly. Dome site continues at V51.1 last_updated 2026-04-17 with no new author-side infrastructure — repo metadata still null. Net position: we now hold a brandable short URL, an apex umbrella, and a 301-preserving legacy property; dome still holds a longer path, a null repo About panel, and the content-volume AI-steering moat (daily_review.json, llms-full.txt, ai_manifest.json agent_rules) we explicitly decline to replicate. This is the strongest competitive shift since the 2026-04-12 GSC verification.

actions_taken.action: Verified 301 redirect from legacy canonical to new canonical
actions_taken.reasoning: curl -I https://funwithscience-org.github.io/dome-model-review/ returns HTTP 301 with location https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/. Ideal behavior — preserves any inbound link equity accumulated during the github.io phase. Confirms operator-note statement.

actions_taken.action: Verified apex and sub-path live at the new canonical
actions_taken.reasoning: curl -I https://funwithscience.net/ → 200; curl -I https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ → 200. HTTPS enforced. last-modified 2026-04-21 on both — GitHub Pages served the build today.

funwithscience.net/sitemap.xml lists 2 URLs (apex + dome-review, both lastmod 2026-04-21). funwithscience.net/robots.txt lists BOTH sitemaps (apex + sub-path). funwithscience.net/llms.txt is a clean umbrella pointing to the dome-review llms.txt and sitemap. No AI-steering patterns introduced — matches the standard 'llms.txt as descriptive index' approach we use on the sub-site.

actions_taken.action: Verified sub-site machine-readable surfaces updated for migration
actions_taken.reasoning: docs/index.html: canonical link, og:url, ClaimReview 'url', ClaimReview 'author.url' all = https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/. itemReviewed.url still = https://john09289.github.io/predictions (correct — that's the subject of review). docs/sitemap.xml: 2 URLs both on new canonical. docs/robots.txt: Sitemap line on new canonical. docs/llms.txt: URL-invariant content (survived migration byte-identical). No stale github.io strings in the machine-readable layer.

actions_taken.action: Checked operator-hand items via auto-detection (SOCIAL-NOTE-003 protocol)
actions_taken.reasoning: GSC legacy property: DONE (file previously committed 2026-04-12; working tree shows absence locally but that's a clean-clone artifact, not a regression). GSC new property (funwithscience.net Domain): NOT YET DONE. Bing Webmaster: PENDING (DNS TXT retry tomorrow). GitHub repo description + 6 topics: DONE (unchanged). GitHub repo homepage: STALE — still on github.io URL. IndexNow key file: PENDING.

Set primary our_site.url to the new canonical. Added url_history capturing the legacy URL with role='former canonical' and the 301 behavior note. Added apex_landing block documenting the umbrella surface. Added migration_notes block. Marked github_repo_metadata.homepage as stale. Updated gsc_verification, bing_indexnow, and notes to reflect today's state. Rebuilt gap_analysis — moved 'brandable short URL' and 'apex umbrella' from gaps to our_persistent_advantages; added four migration-specific new_gaps_opened items (GSC new property, Bing verification, repo homepage, ClaimReview validator re-check).

actions_taken.action: Appended migration-focused search-rankings run and added migration_note to the file header
actions_taken.reasoning: First site:funwithscience.net snapshot establishes the new tracking baseline (Day 0). Retained site:funwithscience-org.github.io for the transition window so we can observe the legacy property drift out of index over the next few weeks. Surfaced the one existing funwithscience.net indexed page (wimshurst-machine legacy product listing — prior occupant of the domain, unrelated to dome content, not a concern) so it is on record rather than a surprise.

Added status=consumed + consumed_at + action_summary to SOCIAL-NOTE-004 per the consume-pattern used for earlier notes. Per CLAUDE.md Human Notes Rule and SOCIAL-NOTE-004 reporting rule, social does NOT commit docs/ or git-owned files — the decider owns commits. Social's path reads from the FUSE workspace; this write is sufficient for social's own future runs, and the decider will pick up the consumed status on its next clone+pull.

issues_filed.summary: Carry-forward from run1. Part 2b (SEC-2b.1) prose in data/sections.json still contains hardcoded '69/(69+4+8) ≈ 85.2%'. Should be 69/(69+4+10) ≈ 83.1% (uncounted-failures.json entries.length=10 after FAIL-010). Drift now two-step gap from data. Concrete draft at monitor/social/drafts/sections-json-2b1-formula-patch.md with preferred template-token fix and fallback manual refresh. NOT re-filed as a new issue — this is a re-flag of the run1 issue, not a duplicate.

human_decisions_needed.question: GSC Domain property verification on funwithscience.net — next available operator window?
human_decisions_needed.context: Operator-hand item 1 in the checklist. Blocks us from submitting the sitemap to Google for the new canonical and from URL Inspection indexing pushes. Until this is done, the old canonical's GSC property (legacy) is still receiving redirect signals but the new canonical is essentially invisible to active crawl-hint channels. Expected first-index window for the new canonical begins when this is done. Rough time: ~15–20 min. Counterargument: if GA4 pageview data already shows meaningful organic arrivals before GSC-new is set up, the migration may be naturally indexing via the 301 alone and the GSC push is lower-value — but GA4 won't tell you about impressions (only clicks), so you'd miss the 'impressions but no clicks' signal that GSC uniquely surfaces.

human_decisions_needed.question: Bing Webmaster Tools — retry DNS TXT tomorrow as planned?
human_decisions_needed.context: Operator-hand item 2. Retry planned. If DNS TXT fails again, we can draft an HTML-meta verification patch (msvalidate.01 meta tag in docs/index.html) — flag in a human-note and social will draft it. Counterargument: Bing is ~5–8% US share; low leverage unless the HTML-meta path also fails, in which case it's a signal that DNS propagation at GoDaddy has a systematic issue worth separate investigation.

human_decisions_needed.question: GitHub repo homepage field update — now or batched with other operator tasks?
human_decisions_needed.context: Operator-hand item 4. One gh API call or one web-UI edit. Low urgency (301 preserves inbound traffic) but stale metadata is a small credibility signal to anyone inspecting the repo About panel. Counterargument: batching with GSC verification keeps context-switching cost down; no functional harm to waiting a day.

human_decisions_needed.question: ClaimReview schema validator re-check against new canonical — when?
human_decisions_needed.context: Operator-hand item 6. ~2 min at validator.schema.org. Only url/author.url changed; itemReviewed.url still points at the dome site (correct). Low-risk. Counterargument: validator.schema.org is sometimes flaky and returns false-negative errors; if it errors on the ClaimReview fetch, don't patch reflexively — file a note and social will investigate whether the error is real or validator-side.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome sitemap has 25 URLs vs our combined 4 (2 apex + 2 dome-review)
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Our review is one canonical HTML page plus llms.txt per site; splitting for URL count is SEO padding. ClaimReview JSON-LD carries structured-data consumers. Migration did not change this stance.
Core AI-steering pattern we explicitly reject. Unchanged.

Our llms.txt is 59 lines and covers full methodology + OTS wrong-side error + 10/9/1 three-way prospective_confirmed disagreement + documentary-vs-structural distinction. Split risks drift. Revisit past 200 lines.

no_action_gaps.gap: Legacy wimshurst-machine page on funwithscience.net is indexed; our dome review path is not (yet)
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Unrelated legacy page at /product/wimshurst-machine/ — prior occupant of the domain. Distinct URL path, won't confuse dome-model queries. Not worth any action; will simply be joined by our indexed dome-review page once Google recrawls after GSC Domain verification + sitemap submission.

issues_for_decider.issue: Carry-forward from run1. Hardcoded '69/(69+4+8) ≈ 85.2%' in the SEC-2b.1 'monitoring illusion' paragraph should be 69/(69+4+10) ≈ 83.1% (uncounted-failures.json entries.length=10 after FAIL-010). Drifts from og:description which correctly shows '10 acknowledged failures'. CLAUDE.md forbids hardcoded derivable counts. Same pattern as ISS-831 (fixed 2026-04-12 by re-hardcoding rather than templating) and ISS-1076 (fixed 2026-04-17).
issues_for_decider.suggested_fix: See monitor/social/drafts/sections-json-2b1-formula-patch.md — preferred template-token fix replaces with {{TOTAL_WINS}}/({{TOTAL_WINS}}+{{DOME_CLAIMED_FAILURES}}+{{UNCOUNTED_FAILURE_COUNT}}) ≈ {{INCL_FAILURES_ACCURACY_PCT}} and adds two tokens to build-scripts/generate-html.js templateVars; fallback is one-line manual refresh.

auto_detection.gsc_verification_legacy_property: DONE (funwithscience-org.github.io; keep verified for redirect-signal collection)
auto_detection.gsc_verification_new_property: NOT YET DONE (funwithscience.net Domain property needed; operator-hand item 1)
auto_detection.bing_webmaster_tools: PENDING (DNS TXT retry tomorrow; operator-hand item 2)

discoverability.indexing_window_status: RESET. The 2026-04-19–04-26 window was for the github.io canonical; that window is superseded by the migration. New window begins when the operator completes GSC Domain property verification on funwithscience.net — a realistic first-index expectation for the new canonical is 7–14 days from that verification + sitemap submission + URL Inspection push. The legacy github.io property will drift out of index as Google consolidates signals via the 301.

counter_arguments.note: Per steve's standing preference, considering the other side.
counter_arguments.on_the_migration_being_a_net_positive: Counter-case: a domain migration can trigger a 2–6 week indexing limbo where the legacy property is deindexing faster than the new property is gaining authority — net discoverability can dip before it rises. If we had any organic traffic before today (we did not — zero indexed pages across both canonicals), the migration would be a net cost in the short term. Response: we had zero indexed pages on the legacy canonical, so there is no short-term loss to absorb. The migration cost is 0 and the ceiling is higher (brandable URL, apex umbrella, 301 equity transfer when/if inbound links accumulate). Net positive even under the pessimistic reading.
counter_arguments.on_keeping_the_legacy_gsc_property_verif: Counter-case: keeping the legacy property verified indefinitely costs nothing but also yields diminishing returns after Google consolidates via the 301 (typically 2–3 months). After consolidation, the legacy property will mostly report zero impressions and be noise. Response: keeping it verified costs zero operator effort after initial setup and the upside (catching any residual legacy-URL link traffic + observing the consolidation drift) is free. The noise is fine; we don't need to look at it daily.
counter_arguments.on_operator_hand_checklist_framing: Counter-case: a 4-item operator checklist can feel like busywork and might get deferred. Response: items are prioritized (high/medium/low) with rough times (~20, ~10, ~1, ~2 min), and only item 1 (GSC Domain property) is truly on the critical path for re-establishing a first-index window. Items 2, 4, 6 are all low-urgency — the checklist format is for convenience, not pressure. If operator has 15 min this week, item 1 is the only must-do.
counter_arguments.on_waiting_for_gsc_verification_before_f: Counter-case: if we don't hear from Google for weeks after GSC-new verification, the default reaction is to add more SEO infrastructure or push more signals — which risks overfitting to Google's crawl cadence at the expense of other priorities. Response: the current discipline is fine — log search-rankings daily, check auto-detection, don't escalate until the canonical 7–14 day window passes post-verification. Resist the urge to add AI-steering patterns just because the dome did.
counter_arguments.on_the_legacy_wimshurst_page: Counter-case: having a Google-indexed unrelated legacy page on the same domain COULD confuse new users who land there and expect dome-model content. Response: the URL path (/product/wimshurst-machine/) is distinct from /dome-model-review/, and it only surfaces under queries about physics education products — no plausible dome-model query routes a user there. If it ever becomes an issue, a simple 410 Gone in .htaccess or robots Disallow could scrub it. Not worth acting on today.
